text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Somatic + Germline Tumor/Normal Paired Analysis

========================================================
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
========================================================
Patient Name: Denise Okafor
Date of Birth: [on file]
Ordering Physician: [Breast Medical Oncology]
Institution: [Medical Center Oncology Program]

Tumor Specimen:
  Specimen ID: TB-2024-OKAFOR-01T
  Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy
  Anatomic Site: Left breast, upper outer quadrant
  Procedure: Core needle biopsy (14-gauge, ultrasound-guided)
  Tumor Histology: Invasive ductal carcinoma, grade 3 (poorly differentiated)
  Tumor Purity (estimated by computational analysis): 58%
  Neoplastic Cell Content (pathologist estimate): 55-60%
  Sections Reviewed: H&E-reviewed prior to nucleic acid extraction; adequate cellularity confirmed

Normal (Germline) Specimen:
  Specimen ID: TB-2024-OKAFOR-01N
  Specimen Type: Peripheral blood (EDTA tube)
  DNA Source: Buffy coat

========================================================
METHODOLOGY
========================================================
Platform: Comprehensive genomic profiling using hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing (paired tumor/normal)
Panel Coverage: ~600-gene targeted panel covering all coding exons of cancer-relevant genes, including selected introns for fusion detection
Sequencing Depth (mean on-target):
  Tumor: 682x (median 650x)
  Normal: 310x (median 295x)
Variant Calling: Somatic SNVs/indels called using paired tumor-normal subtraction pipeline; germline variants called from normal specimen
Copy Number Analysis: Whole-exome-level copy number from on-target + off-target read depth, normalized to matched normal and panel-of-normals reference
Fusion Detection: Anchored multiplex PCR and split-read analysis of selected intronic regions covering known fusion partners (ALK, RET, NTRK1/2/3, ROS1, FGFR2/3, NRG1, EGFR, MET)
TMB Calculation: Somatic nonsynonymous mutations per megabase across ~1.1 Mb coding sequence (synonymous and germline-filtered)
MSI Analysis: Fragment analysis of 30 microsatellite loci; computational MSI score derived from sequencing data
HRD Analysis: Genomic scarring composite score (sum of loss of heterozygosity [LOH] segments >15 Mb + large-scale state transitions [LST] + telomeric allelic imbalance [TAI]); genomic LOH fraction separately reported
Germline Pathogenicity Classification: ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines; variant classified as Pathogenic, Likely Pathogenic, VUS, Likely Benign, or Benign
Analysis Limitations: This assay detects the majority of clinically actionable substitutions, insertions/deletions, copy number alterations, and select fusions; it does not detect all structural rearrangements, epigenetic alterations, or variants in non-covered regions; sensitivity for variants present at <5% VAF may be reduced

========================================================
BIOMARKER SUMMARY
========================================================
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):    11 mutations/Mb    [INTERMEDIATE; range 0-62 in this tumor type]
Microsatellite Status (MSI):      MSS (microsatellite stable)
PD-L1 CPS (22C3, IHC):           18    [Positive; confirmed by immunohistochemistry, reported separately]
HRD Composite Score:              68    [POSITIVE; institutional cutoff >42]
Genomic Loss of Heterozygosity:   22%   [Elevated; cutoff for HRD-positive >16%]
Homologous Recombination Status:  HRD-POSITIVE (by genomic scarring)
Germline Pathogenic Variant:      BRCA2 c.5946delT (p.Ser1982ArgfsTer22) -- PATHOGENIC

========================================================
GENOMIC ALTERATIONS IDENTIFIED
========================================================

--- SOMATIC ALTERATIONS ---

Gene        | Alteration                          | VAF   | CNA   | Classification      | Functional Consequence
------------|-------------------------------------|-------|-------|---------------------|------------------------
TP53        | p.R248W (c.742C>T)                  | 42%   | --    | Pathogenic          | Loss of function (dominant negative); hotspot mutation, DNA contact residue; abrogates sequence-specific DNA binding
PIK3CA      | p.H1047R (c.3140A>G)               | 18%   | --    | Pathogenic          | Gain of function; exon 20 kinase domain hotspot; activates PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway
RB1         | Splice site c.1386+1G>A             | 31%   | --    | Likely Pathogenic   | Loss of function; canonical splice donor disruption, predicted to result in exon 12 skipping and frameshift
PTEN        | p.R130Q (c.389G>A)                  | 24%   | --    | Likely Pathogenic   | Loss of function; phosphatase domain mutation; reduced lipid phosphatase activity; contributes to PI3K pathway hyperactivation
RAD51C      | p.L138F (c.412C>T)                  | 15%   | --    | Variant of Uncertain Significance (VUS) | Missense in RAD51C coiled-coil domain; functional impact on HRR uncertain; note: germline status not confirmed for this variant; likely somatic

Gene        | Alteration                          | Copy Number       | Classification      | Functional Consequence
------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------|---------------------|------------------------
MYC         | Amplification                       | ~12 copies (gain) | Pathogenic          | Oncogene amplification; 8q24.21; transcriptional upregulation of proliferative gene programs
CCNE1       | Amplification                       | ~6 copies (gain)  | Pathogenic          | Oncogene amplification; 19q12; cyclin E1 overexpression drives CDK2-mediated S-phase entry, associated with replication stress
CDKN2A      | Homozygous deletion                 | 0 copies          | Pathogenic          | Tumor suppressor loss; 9p21.3; loss of both p16(INK4a) and p14(ARF) transcripts; deregulation of CDK4/6-RB and MDM2-p53 axes

--- GERMLINE ALTERATIONS ---

Gene        | Alteration                          | Zygosity          | Classification      | Notes
------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------|---------------------|-------
BRCA2       | c.5946delT (p.Ser1982ArgfsTer22)    | Heterozygous      | PATHOGENIC          | Frameshift deletion; loss of function; truncating variant in exon 11; absent from gnomAD non-cancer cohort; ClinVar: Pathogenic (multiple submitters); LOVD: class 5; well-characterized Fanconi anemia complementation group D1 associated allele; results in protein truncation upstream of DNA-binding domain; functional HRR impairment confirmed in literature

--- ALTERATIONS NOT DETECTED ---
EGFR: no mutation
KRAS: no mutation
NRAS: no mutation
BRAF: no mutation
CDH1: no mutation
AKT1: no mutation
ERBB2 (HER2): no amplification; no activating mutation (consistent with IHC 1+, FISH non-amplified)
MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2: no pathogenic variants detected (consistent with MSS phenotype)
NTRK1/NTRK2/NTRK3: no fusion detected
ALK: no fusion detected
RET: no fusion detected
ROS1: no fusion detected
FGFR2: no fusion or amplification detected
ESR1: no mutation
PIK3R1: no mutation

========================================================
INTERPRETATION
========================================================
This tumor demonstrates a genomic profile characteristic of high-grade, triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-) invasive ductal carcinoma with features of genomic instability and homologous recombination deficiency (HRD). The findings are interpreted in the context of the patient's clinical diagnosis of Stage IIB TNBC.

Key Interpretive Points:

1. GERMLINE BRCA2 PATHOGENIC VARIANT: A frameshift deletion in BRCA2 (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982ArgfsTer22) was confirmed in peripheral blood (germline origin). This is a well-characterized loss-of-function variant that abolishes BRCA2 function in homologous recombination repair (HRR). In the tumor, somatic loss of the wild-type BRCA2 allele is inferred based on the elevated HRD composite score (68) and gLOH (22%), consistent with biallelic functional inactivation. This germline finding has significant implications for the patient and first-degree relatives (50% transmission risk) and warrants formal genetic counseling. In addition to breast cancer risk (lifetime risk ~71% for BRCA2 carriers), this variant confers substantially elevated ovarian cancer risk (lifetime risk ~17-20%), as well as increased risks for pancreatic, prostate (in male relatives), and other cancers.

2. HRD-POSITIVE GENOMIC PROFILE: The HRD composite score of 68 (positive; cutoff >42) and gLOH of 22% indicate extensive genomic scarring consistent with a defect in homologous recombination repair. This is mechanistically explained by the germline BRCA2 variant combined with the somatic RAD51C missense VUS (significance uncertain). HRD positivity has therapeutic implications, particularly for sensitivity to platinum-based chemotherapy and PARP inhibitors.

3. TP53 p.R248W (VAF 42%): This is among the most common and well-characterized TP53 hotspot mutations, occurring at the DNA contact arginine-248 codon. The R248W substitution not only abolishes wild-type p53 tumor suppressor function but also confers a dominant-negative gain-of-function effect, interfering with residual wild-type p53 activity and promoting tumor aggressiveness, chromosomal instability, and resistance to apoptosis. High VAF (42%) suggests clonal, early event. Consistent with grade 3 histology and 85% Ki-67.

4. PIK3CA p.H1047R (VAF 18%): This is a canonical activating hotspot in the kinase domain of PIK3CA (PI3K catalytic subunit alpha), resulting in constitutive PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling. Present at sub-clonal VAF (18%), suggesting possible emergence in a subpopulation or selection under prior microenvironmental pressures. In TNBC, PIK3CA mutations may co-occur with PTEN loss (see below), compounding pathway activation.

5. RB1 Splice Site Mutation + CDKN2A Homozygous Deletion: Co-occurring RB1 loss-of-function (splice donor disruption) and homozygous CDKN2A deletion represent dual deregulation of the CDK4/6-RB cell cycle axis. Loss of both p16(INK4a) and p14(ARF) encoded by CDKN2A eliminates negative regulation of CDK4/6 (via p16) and MDM2 (via p14ARF), the latter further impairing residual p53 pathway function. Together, these alterations predict an aggressive proliferative phenotype consistent with grade 3 histology and extremely elevated Ki-67 (85%).

6. PTEN p.R130Q (VAF 24%): Missense mutation in the phosphatase domain of PTEN reduces or eliminates lipid phosphatase activity, impairing conversion of PIP3 to PIP2 and leading to unopposed PI3K/AKT pathway activation. Co-occurrence with PIK3CA p.H1047R suggests strong selection for PI3K pathway hyper-activation in this tumor.

7. MYC Amplification (copy number ~12): High-level MYC amplification at 8q24.21 drives broad transcriptional programs supporting cell growth, proliferation, metabolism, and immune evasion. Frequently observed in high-grade TNBC. Associated with aggressive biology and poor prognosis in the neoadjuvant setting.

8. CCNE1 Amplification (copy number ~6): Cyclin E1 amplification promotes CDK2-mediated S-phase entry and has been associated with replication stress, genomic instability, and relative resistance to CDK4/6 inhibitors (which are not standard of care in TNBC). May have implications for emerging CDK2-targeted therapies.

9. RAD51C p.L138F (VAF 15%, VUS): Missense variant in RAD51C (a BRCA2-interacting protein critical to RAD51 filament formation). Classified as VUS due to insufficient functional and population data. Given the already-established HRD phenotype driven by germline BRCA2 loss, the clinical significance of this somatic VUS is less determinative. No germline RAD51C pathogenic variant identified.

10. TMB 11 mut/Mb (Intermediate): Intermediate TMB. Not classified as TMB-high (generally defined as >=10 mut/Mb in some guidelines, but clinical significance of intermediate TMB in breast cancer remains investigational). PD-L1 CPS 18 (positive) is the more clinically relevant immunotherapy biomarker in this context.

11. MSS: Absence of microsatellite instability is consistent with mismatch repair-proficient tumor. MMR gene alterations not identified.

========================================================
CLINICAL ACTIONABILITY SUMMARY
========================================================

TIER I -- STRONG CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE (FDA-approved therapy/guideline-concordant)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Biomarker                          | Drug(s)                              | Setting / Evidence
-----------------------------------|--------------------------------------|-----------------------------
Germline BRCA2 Pathogenic Variant  | ola (Lynparza)                  | Adjuvant TNBC with high-risk residual disease (OlympiA trial; FDA-approved); also active in metastatic HER2-negative breast cancer (OlympiAD)
                                   | tala (Talzenna)               | Metastatic HER2-negative gBRCA1/2 breast cancer (FDA-approved)
                                   | Platinum agents (carbo,        | HRD-positive TNBC; preferential sensitivity to platinum in germline BRCA carriers
                                   |   cis)                         |
PD-L1 CPS 18 (positive, CPS >=10) | Keytruda (Keytruda)             | Neoadjuvant + adjuvant TNBC, Stage II-III (KEYNOTE-522; FDA-approved); metastatic TNBC, 1L (KEYNOTE-355)
HRD-Positive (score 68)            | ola, tala                | Converges with gBRCA2 tier above; HRD score supports platinum/PARP sensitivity

TIER II -- POTENTIAL CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE (Off-label / emerging evidence)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Biomarker                          | Drug(s)                              | Evidence Level
-----------------------------------|--------------------------------------|-----------------------------
PIK3CA p.H1047R + PTEN loss        | PI3K/AKT/mTOR inhibitors             | Alpelisib (PIK3CA-specific) approved in HR+/HER2- with PIK3CA mut; limited data in TNBC; capivasertib + fulvestrant (not applicable in TNBC); investigational in TNBC context
                                   | evero (mTOR)                    | Investigational combinations; not standard in TNBC
CCNE1 Amplification                | CDK2 inhibitors (investigational)    | Phase I/II data emerging; no approved agent
MYC Amplification                  | BET bromodomain inhibitors           | Preclinical/Phase I; no approved agent
CDKN2A Deletion                    | CDK4/6 inhibitors                    | Limited evidence in TNBC (RB1 also lost, likely rendering CDK4/6 inhibition ineffective; CDK4/6i not standard in TNBC)

TIER III -- UNCERTAIN CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE
--------------------------------------------
RAD51C p.L138F                     | VUS; no current actionability; may warrant reassessment as functional data accrue
TP53 p.R248W                       | No directly approved targeted agent; dominant-negative TP53 mutations have been investigated in APR-246/eprenetapopt trials (Phase III failure in MDS/AML; limited breast cancer data)

NOT ACTIONABLE (alterations detected but no current therapeutic implications)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RB1 splice site c.1386+1G>A: loss of RB1 in context of concurrent CDKN2A deletion; RB1 loss predicts resistance to CDK4/6 inhibitors (not relevant in current TNBC standard-of-care context)
TMB 11 mut/Mb: intermediate; Keytruda approved for TMB-high (>=10 mut/Mb) solid tumors (FDA, tumor-agnostic); PD-L1 CPS the more relevant biomarker here

========================================================
GERMLINE DISCLOSURE NOTE
========================================================
A PATHOGENIC GERMLINE VARIANT has been identified in this patient:
  Gene: BRCA2
  Variant: c.5946delT (p.Ser1982ArgfsTer22)
  Classification: PATHOGENIC
  Inheritance: Autosomal dominant

This result has been communicated to the ordering physician. Per institutional policy and NSGC/ACMG guidelines, genetic counseling referral is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED. The patient and first-degree blood relatives (children, siblings, parents) may be at substantially elevated risk for breast, ovarian, pancreatic, and other cancers. Cascade genetic testing is available for at-risk family members.

Result has been flagged for communication to the genetic counselor on file.

========================================================
REPORT ATTESTATION
========================================================
This report was reviewed and approved by:
  [Molecular Pathologist, MD/PhD]
  [Laboratory Director, CLIA Certified Molecular Oncology Laboratory]
  CLIA #: [on file]
  CAP Accredited: Yes

Result Classification Key:
  Pathogenic: Established driver/disease-causing alteration per published evidence, functional studies, and/or population databases
  Likely Pathogenic: Strong evidence of functional impact; does not yet meet full pathogenic criteria
  VUS: Insufficient evidence to classify as pathogenic or benign; not currently actionable
  Likely Benign / Benign: Not reported

This report is intended for use by qualified healthcare professionals in conjunction with all clinical information. Genomic findings should be interpreted in the full clinical context. This report does not constitute a complete genetic evaluation.

[END OF REPORT]
